successCriteria.description: src/modules/notification/notification.model.ts exists and re-exports Notification from shared types, plus any module-specific DTOs.
src/modules/notification/notification.repository.ts exists, implements INotificationRepository, and extends BaseRepository<Notification>.
src/modules/notification/notification.service.ts exists, implements INotificationService, and depends on INotificationRepository and IAuditService.
src/modules/notification/notification.controller.ts exists, uses Fastify with Zod validation and RBAC, and handles all async errors (GP-006).
src/modules/notification/notification.routes.ts exists and registers GET /notifications, GET /notifications/:id, PATCH /notifications/:id/read.
Jest unit tests exist in tests/unit/modules/notification/ covering repository, service, and controller.
All state-changing operations (createNotification, sendNotification, markAsRead) write an audit record via IAuditService (GP-002).
When creating or sending a notification, the notification record and its audit record are written in a single database transaction to ensure consistency.
All API endpoints enforce RBAC (GP-005) and validate inputs with Zod (GP-003).

constraints: Repository pattern: All database access goes through repository interfaces.
Audit records: All state-changing operations write an audit record.
Input validation: Validate all inputs at API boundaries before processing.
No sensitive data in logs: Never log passwords, tokens, PII, or financial data.
RBAC enforcement: All API endpoints enforce role-based access control.
Error handling: No unhandled promise rejections. All async errors are caught and handled.
When creating or sending a notification, the notification record and its audit record are written in a single database transaction.
outOfScope: Any other modules not explicitly mentioned in this phase
